evaluation_meta.notes: Stage 6 validation run. Session correctly identified information asymmetry as a process prerequisite and blocked reimbursement option work until shared document review was agreed. Party B acknowledged asymmetry cooperatively (contrasting with D-B-RT02's adversarial deflection). Stayed in bounded M1 caution — no premature severe escalation.

C3.note: Interests elicited adequately, but Party A's interest in informed participation correctly treated as a process prerequisite rather than a negotiable concern.

C5.note: Option generation correctly blocked pending information disclosure. The blocking was appropriate — no premature option work against unresolved information prerequisite.

C6.note: Information access framed as foundational to informed participation — not as accusation or tactic. Party B's acknowledgment of the asymmetry was treated as constructive, not used as leverage.

C7.note: Perception agent correctly identified Party A's uncertainty and deference pattern. Asymmetry signals surfaced from first party contribution. Dynamic trajectory correctly assessed as stable once Party B acknowledged asymmetry.

C8.note: Correctly deferred substantive decision until information prerequisites were met. Did not push reimbursement commitment from an uninformed position.

C9.note: Correct M1 bounded caution. Did not over-escalate to M2/M3 — the case explicitly tests avoiding premature severe escalation. Information asymmetry acknowledged cooperatively, which appropriately reduces severity threshold.

P3.note: Domain confidence correctly calibrated. Asymmetry identified and held as blocking constraint, but bounded caution chosen over severe escalation. Correctly distinguished cooperative acknowledgment of asymmetry (D-B11) from adversarial deflection (D-B-RT02).

P4.note: No substantive custody options generated — correct. Process-step agreement (shared document review) is the appropriate output when information is the prerequisite.

P6.note: Qualification discipline maintained. Information disclosure framed as non-negotiable prerequisite, not optional adjunct to option work.

I3.note: Asymmetry narrative preserved consistently across trace and flags. The session's outcome — shared document review as next step — is clearly derivable from artifacts.

ES1_detection_accuracy.note: Information asymmetry correctly detected as escalation-sensitive condition at T2.

ES2_threshold_calibration.note: Bounded caution (M1) correctly chosen over severe escalation (M2/M3). Party B's cooperative acknowledgment of asymmetry appropriately reduced severity — not treated as coercive pattern.

ES3_mode_selection_quality.note: M1 is the correct mode. Case explicitly tests avoiding premature severe escalation when asymmetry is cooperatively acknowledged.

escalation_review.notes: The key calibration test for D-B11 is correctly distinguishing cooperative asymmetry acknowledgment from adversarial deflection. This session correctly stayed M1 — bounded caution without premature M2/M3.

PQ1_emotional_state_accuracy.note: Party A's uncertainty and deference correctly identified. Party B's relative confidence and constructive acknowledgment also accurately characterised.

PQ2_interest_inference_quality.note: Party A's interest in informed participation and Party B's interest in moving forward correctly identified. Asymmetry not mis-characterised as adversarial intent.

PQ3_risk_signal_detection.note: Information asymmetry risk correctly detected and treated as a process prerequisite. No over-detection (did not escalate to CATEGORY 2 veto).

PQ4_relational_dynamic_awareness.note: Asymmetric but cooperative dynamic correctly assessed. The key differentiation from RT02 (adversarial deflection) was correctly made.

perception_quality_review.perception_asymmetry_note: Party A (less informed) vs Party B (information-holding) asymmetry correctly detected. Perception agent correctly characterised Party A as deferential/uncertain rather than compliant — important distinction that drove the M1 (not M0) positioning.
perception_quality_review.perception_quality_notes: Stage 6 D-B11 is the cooperative asymmetry benchmark. Perception quality is competent — correctly read the cooperative vs. adversarial distinction that separates this case from D-B-RT02.

OQ1_option_diversity.note: No substantive options generated — correct. The option quality question for D-B11 is the quality of the blocking rationale rather than the pool itself.

OQ2_interest_alignment.note: Options correctly blocked pending information prerequisite. Interest alignment cannot be assessed without the information both parties need.

OQ3_domain_fit.note: Information disclosure as a prerequisite to reimbursement options is correct family law domain practice. Domain reasoner correctly applied the prerequisite logic.

OQ4_non_obvious_value.note: Pool not generated — correct. N/A in context.

OQ5_qualification_accuracy.note: All options correctly blocked with accurate, specific blocking rationales citing the information prerequisite. Blocking was not premature or over-broad — it correctly targeted the specific information gap.

option_quality_review.option_quality_notes: D-B11 option quality is correctly 'developing' band — the key quality signal is OQ3 and OQ5 (domain fit and qualification accuracy), not diversity or pool depth. The stage 6 quality claim for D-B11 is that the blocking logic was correctly applied and the information-prerequisite rationale was well-articulated.

final_judgment.short_rationale: Correctly identified information asymmetry as a process prerequisite, maintained bounded M1 caution without premature severe escalation, and reached shared agreement on document review as the next step. The key D-B11 test — distinguishing cooperative asymmetry from adversarial deflection — passed.
final_judgment.follow_up_recommendation: Use D-B11-S07 as the Stage 6 cooperative asymmetry anchor. Pair with D-B-RT02-S04 (adversarial deflection) to test the calibration difference between the two asymmetry patterns.
